text.title: Kubernetes Cluster Backup with Velero Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: New 2026 cluster installed with `--uploader-type=restic`; backups are 5x slower and Velero docs warn the path is deprecated.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied a 2023 install command without checking the data-mover migration.
antipattern.fix: Re-install with `--uploader-type=kopia`; migrate existing repos via Velero's documented data-mover migration script.

antipattern.symptom: Postgres restore from a Velero CSI snapshot fails to start: `LOG: invalid checkpoint record`. Snapshot was crash-consistent but not application-consistent.

root-cause.source: Velero hooks docs, PostgreSQL crash-recovery docs

antipattern.fix: Add pre-hook `pg_start_backup` (or `SELECT pg_backup_start()` on PG 15+) and post-hook `pg_stop_backup`. Test restore in a temp namespace.

antipattern.symptom: Cluster in eu-central-1 backs up to an S3 bucket in eu-central-1; regional outage takes both down simultaneously.
antipattern.root-cause: Backup storage location defaulted to cluster's home region.
antipattern.fix: Provision a second BackupStorageLocation in a different region (eu-west-1 or us-east-1) and set it as the schedule target.
